fix(ci): move PR report to workflow_run for fork PR support (#225)

* Initial plan

* fix: add pull-requests write permissions to GitHub Actions workflows

Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): remove ineffective job-level permissions from reusable workflow

* fix(ci): pass PR write permission from caller to reusable unit-tests workflow

* fix(ci): harden CI/CD workflows with security fixes and reliability improvements

- Pin all actions to commit SHAs to prevent supply-chain attacks
- Fix shell injection in ci-integration.yml by using env vars instead of direct interpolation
- Scope permissions per-job in publish.yml (was granting pull-requests:write to publish job)
- Restrict claude-code-review to trusted contributors only (OWNER/MEMBER/COLLABORATOR)
- Switch claude-code-review to pull_request_target for fork PR support
- Fix fail-fast: false in ci-unit-tests.yml cross-platform matrix
- Remove duplicate ubuntu-latest from unit test matrix
- Add timeouts to all workflow jobs
- Improve kuzu-db test loop to continue on failure and report per-file errors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): read thresholds from `vitest.config.ts`

* fix(ci): move PR report to workflow_run for fork PR support

The sticky-pull-request-comment and vitest-coverage-report-action
both fail on fork PRs because pull_request events receive a read-only
GITHUB_TOKEN. This extracts PR reporting into a separate ci-report.yml
workflow triggered by workflow_run, which always gets read/write tokens.

Changes:
- ci.yml: replace pr-report job with save-pr-meta artifact upload
- ci-unit-tests.yml: remove davelosert/vitest-coverage-report-action,
  add coverage-final.json to artifact for merging
- ci-integration.yml: add ubuntu coverage job for non-kuzu groups
- ci-report.yml (new): workflow_run handler that downloads artifacts,
  merges unit + integration coverage via Istanbul, and posts combined
  PR comment with sticky-pull-request-comment

* feat(ci): show unit, integration, and merged coverage in PR report

- Disable coverage thresholds for integration-only run (partial coverage)
- Display combined coverage as the primary metric
- Show per-suite breakdown (unit / integration) in expandable details
- Thresholds applied against combined coverage, not individual suites

* fix(ci): add coverage collection input for PR reports and validate job results

* fix(ci): refine Claude Code Review workflow to support issue comments and enhance trusted contributor checks

feat: activate SPA mode with BMW WebSocket transport (#1351)

Wire up the existing VNext SPA infrastructure that was built but never
connected:

SPA Activation:
- Fix ID mismatch: vnext-app.js looked for #vnext-root but server
  created #vnext-content — now tries both
- Add #vnext-root div to body template for server-rendered pages
- Hide SSR content (.bm-ssr-content) when VNext SPA activates
- Switch footer from bundle.js (legacy) to vnext-bundle.js (SPA)
- Intercept ALL internal link clicks for SPA navigation (not just
  [data-go]) with exclusion list for /api, /auth, /login, /static

BMW WebSocket Transport in BareMetalRest.js:
- Add connectWs() — opens WebSocket to /bmw/ws with binary frames
- Add wsSend() — 6-byte BMW frame encoding with response correlation
- Fetch /bmw/protocol on init() to build opcode lookup map
- Entity CRUD ops try WebSocket first, fall back to BSO1/JSON
- _sdkName() resolves verb+slug to BMW SDK method names
- _tryWs() attempts WebSocket dispatch, returns null if unavailable
- Expose connectWs(), isWsReady() in public API

Tests:
- 124 JS tests (was 119) — 5 new WebSocket transport tests
- All 24 protocol descriptor C# tests pass

commit.message:
feat: strict mode, WeakRef, Object.prototype.toString conformance (#449)

* Enforce strict mode semantics for undeclared vars, this binding, params, and octals

- StaGlobal/StaLookupSlot: throw ReferenceError when assigning to
  undeclared variables in strict mode instead of creating globals
- CallUndefinedReceiver0/1/2: set 'this' to undefined for strict
  callee functions (save/restore around shared global_env)
- Parser: reject duplicate simple parameter names in strict mode
  functions (checked after body parse so 'use strict' directives work)
- Parser: reject legacy octal literals (e.g. 0123) in strict mode
  (0o123 is still allowed)
- Scanner: expose source() getter for raw token text inspection
- Existing 'with' statement and 'delete variable' checks already present

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(builtins): use Weak<T> for WeakRef/FinalizationRegistry PlainObject targets

WeakRef:
- Add WeakTarget enum supporting both Rc-managed (Weak<RefCell<PropertyMap>>)
  and GC-managed (*mut HeapObject) targets
- Add weak_ref_new_plain() for PlainObject targets using Rc::downgrade()
- deref() now returns Undefined automatically when Rc target is collected
- Constructor in install_globals accepts both Object and PlainObject

FinalizationRegistry:
- Add PlainRegistration with Weak<RefCell<PropertyMap>> target tracking
- Add finalization_registry_register_plain() for PlainObject targets
- Add finalization_registry_unregister_plain() for PlainObject tokens
- Add finalization_registry_sweep_plain() to detect collected Rc targets
- cleanupSome() now sweeps plain targets before draining the queue
- register/unregister in install_globals accept both Object and PlainObject

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement spec-compliant Object.prototype.toString (§20.1.3.6)

- Add JsValue::obj_to_string_tag() that returns the correct [object X]
  string for all value types per ECMAScript spec
- Install Object.prototype.toString on the prototype object
- Update proto_lookup toString fallback to use obj_to_string_tag
- Fix NativeFunction .call()/.apply()/.bind() to pass thisArg through
  so Object.prototype.toString.call(value) works correctly
- Add __is_array__ marker to array literal PlainObjects for detection
- Add @@toStringTag = 'Date' to Date instances
- Handles: Undefined, Null, Boolean, Number, String, Symbol, BigInt,
  Array, Function, Error, Generator, RegExp, Date, Promise, and
  @@toStringTag custom tags

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ml): R4 deep audit — QLSTM scheduler state in checkpoints + resume, Transformer NaN/Inf loss guard

Fix 1: QLSTM checkpoints now include scheduler_state_dict so
CosineAnnealingWarmRestarts resumes correctly after spot eviction.
Also adds checkpoint resume logic (periodic/eviction) to avoid
restarting training from scratch on preempted jobs.

backward() against NaN/Inf loss and skips optimizer.step() on
NaN/Inf gradient norms — parity with LSTM & QLSTM loops.

fix(orleans): R4 deep audit — grain call filter error/histogram metrics, stale position pruning, gradient age eviction

Fix 1: Grain call filter — added goldaig.silo.grain_errors counter with
grain.type/error.type tags and grain_call_duration histogram for p50/p95/p99
latency percentiles. Previously errors had zero OTel metrics.

Fix 2: RiskManagementGrain — added OpenedAtUtc to RiskOpenPosition with
schema v2 migration, plus 30-min timer-based stale position pruning (>7 days).
Prevents phantom positions from API disconnects accumulating below 2x cap.

Fix 3: MLTrainingGrain — added age-based gradient eviction (>24h) on
activation and during SubmitGradientsAsync. Stale gradients from inactive
contributors no longer pollute FedAvg aggregation under the 1000-entry FIFO cap.

produces invalid zodiac sign index and signEnd=390, breaking the applying-aspect
range check at the Pisces/Aries boundary (false VoC positive -> -5.0 penalty).

backtest on the EsotericFeatureProvider hot path.

with flat PlanetNamesByIdFast[planetId] array (O(1) indexing) for cold-path
usages (interpretation, debug logging).

commit.message:
docs(*): add bootstrap step to `copilot-instructions.md` (#517)

The Copilot instructions did not tell agents to bootstrap the workspace
before running validation or making package changes. In a fresh clone,
that leads to avoidable failures and wasted setup time.

- **Summary**
- Add explicit bootstrap guidance to `.github/copilot-instructions.md`.
- Place it directly before the existing test/build/lint workflow so the
install requirement is seen before any validation commands.

- **Changes**
  - Introduce a new `Bootstrapping` section in the Copilot instructions.
- Require `npm install` from the repo root before tests, builds, lint,
or implementation work.
- Clarify that work should not start until install completes
successfully because workspace dependencies are shared across the
monorepo.

- **Resulting guidance**
  ```md
  ## Bootstrapping

- In a fresh clone, run `npm install` from the repo root before
attempting tests, builds, lint, or package changes.
- Do not start validation or implementation work until the install has
completed successfully, because the workspace dependencies are required
across the monorepo.
  ```

commit.message:
feat: add two-tier group isolation to all read methods in async_neo4j_service

All READ methods now accept an optional `group_ids: List[str]` parameter
and compute `effective_ids = group_ids or [group_id, "__global__"]`.

MATCH-based queries: changed `= $group_id` to `IN $effective_ids` and
pass `effective_ids=effective_ids` in params.

VECTOR INDEX SEARCH queries: converted to UNION ALL pattern querying
both `$group_id` and `$global_group_id="__global__"` separately (vector
index WHERE clause only supports single equality).

PPR builder methods (_build_ppr_query_entity_only,
_build_ppr_query_with_section_graph, _build_ppr_query_weighted_section_graph):
all inline `group_id` Cypher variables renamed to `effective_ids` and
all `= group_id` changed to `IN effective_ids`.

Updated methods:
- get_entities_by_importance
- get_entities_by_names (4 sub-queries)
- get_entities_by_vector_similarity (UNION ALL)
- expand_neighbors

commit.message:
feat(script): add raw format and large-file warnings before upload

harvester-vm.sh now runs three checks before handing off to Terraform:

1. Raw format — detected via qemu-img info (authoritative) falling back
   to file extension (.raw, .img). Warns that raw images transfer every
   allocated block and shows the qemu-img convert command to qcow2.

2. Sparse file — existing check (logical > 5x physical). Updated to use
   a per-file outbase path in the suggested convert commands instead of
   the generic 'image.qcow2' placeholder.

3. Large file (> 2 GiB) — warns that upload will take several minutes
   and recommends qcow2 conversion if the format is not already qcow2.

Update README: replace the sparse-file-only section with a table-based
'Image format and size warnings' section covering all three conditions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
